etymology.description: 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + 作(作) (zuò, "make; work") combine to form 手作(手作), meaning "handmade".
etymology.interpretation: 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") refers to the direct involvement of the hands in creating something, emphasizing the manual aspect of the process. 作(作) (zuò, "make; work") indicates the act of producing or crafting. Together, 手作(手作) implies creating something by hand, highlighting the traditional or artisanal nature of the work, as opposed to machine-made products.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢手作的礼物(我喜歡手作的禮物)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān shǒu zuò de lǐwù.

examples.chinese: 市场上展示了许多精美的手作饰品(市場上展示了許多精美的手作飾品)。
examples.pinyin: Shìchǎng shàng zhǎnshìle xǔduō jīngměi de shǒu zuò shìpǐn.
examples.english: The market showcased many exquisite handmade accessories.

examples.chinese: 她在周末开设了一个手作工作坊(她在週末開設了一個手作工作坊)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài zhōumò kāishèle yī gè shǒu zuò gōngzuòfāng.
examples.english: She started a handmade workshop on the weekend.
